---
title: "Den"
subtitle: "Docker-based sandbox runtime for AI agents"
---

Den gives AI agents isolated Docker containers to work in. Pre-warms a container pool for instant sandbox creation. REST + WebSocket API.

## Key Features

- **Instant sandboxes** — pre-warmed container pool, claim in milliseconds
- **Two modes** — spot (pooled) for generic work, dedicated (bind mounts) for zero-copy host access
- **Persistent volumes** — release a sandbox, resume later, files are still there
- **Exec backpressure** — per-sandbox concurrency limits prevent resource exhaustion
- **Defunct detection** — crashed containers are detected and clearly reported, resumable
- **Security** — path traversal prevention, SSRF-safe proxy, resource limits per tier

## Quick Start

```bash
# Build the sandbox image
docker build -t den-sandbox:latest -f Dockerfile.sandbox .

# Run den
cargo run -- --config den.toml
```

```bash
# Create a sandbox
curl -X POST localhost:8080/api/v1/sandboxes \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"tier":"default"}'

# Execute a command
curl -X POST localhost:8080/api/v1/sandboxes/$ID/exec \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"cmd":["echo","hello from sandbox"]}'
```

## How It Works

```
Orchestrator (nama, scripts, CI)
  │
  │  REST / WebSocket
  ▼
┌─────────────────────────────┐
│         Den Server          │
│   Axum + Tokio + Bollard    │
│                             │
│  Pool ──▶ Docker Containers │
│  Reaper ──▶ Cleanup         │
│  Scheduler ──▶ Resources    │
└─────────────────────────────┘
  │
  │  Docker Socket
  ▼
┌─────────────────────────────┐
│     Docker Containers       │
│  Ubuntu 24.04 + tools       │
│  /home/sandbox (volume)     │
│  /workspace (bind mount)    │
└─────────────────────────────┘
```

The agent runs **outside** the container. Containers are just "hands" — exec environments. Den manages the lifecycle, the orchestrator translates tool calls into Den API calls.
